observation: All endpoints average 3.1–3.8s response time — 6–8x above typical 500ms production target for public API
evidence: S01 avg=3443ms; S02 avg=3497ms; S03 avg=3359ms; S05 avg=3077ms; S06 avg=3784ms; S09 avg=3454ms
root_cause_hypothesis: PHP-FPM cold path + SQLite/development DB I/O on each request; no opcode-cache warm-up in dev env; local Windows dev machine (not Linux production server)
recommendation: 1) Enable PHP OPcache; 2) Use PostgreSQL connection pooling; 3) Profile with Xdebug/Blackfire to isolate top 3 slow queries; 4) Add HTTP response caching (Redis) for read-heavy API endpoints; 5) Re-test on Linux staging server to separate dev-env overhead

observation: Integration middleware rejection latency 3237ms avg — 62% above 2000ms threshold; SCENARIO FAIL
evidence: S08A avg=3331ms (no-auth); S08B avg=3142ms (bad-token); middleware_overhead_avg=3237ms; threshold=2000ms
root_cause_hypothesis: Middleware chain executes full PHP bootstrap even for rejected requests; no short-circuit before database/service layer; possible eager service provider loading on every request
recommendation: 1) Move auth token validation to earliest-possible middleware with short-circuit return; 2) Avoid loading CatalogReadService/DB connections before auth check; 3) Add integration API route-level middleware cache; 4) Consider Nginx-level JWT pre-validation to reject unauthenticated requests before PHP-FPM

observation: Web catalog page averages 3784ms with 130KB HTML payload — largest response size and slowest API endpoint
evidence: S06 avg=3784ms; avg_body_bytes=130180; S07 avg=3639ms; same payload
root_cause_hypothesis: Full server-side rendered Blade template includes embedded catalog data in a single HTML response; no fragment caching; CDN not configured
recommendation: 1) Enable Blade partial caching via laravel cache tags; 2) Split catalog data fetch from HTML rendering (SPA or turbo-frames); 3) Compress HTML responses with Gzip/Brotli (Nginx); 4) Paginate catalog results on server; 5) CDN for static assets

observation: Throughput 0.26–0.33 rps across all scenarios — severely limiting concurrent user capacity at 1 VU sequential
root_cause_hypothesis: Sequential request model (1 VU) with 3–4s avg latency yields natural ~0.29 rps ceiling; no concurrency in test tool (PowerShell); actual production concurrency would be much higher
recommendation: 1) Full load test with k6 or Locust at 50–100 concurrent VUs to determine actual capacity ceiling; 2) PHP-FPM pool size tuning; 3) Async queue for heavy operations; 4) Database read replicas for catalog queries

observation: /api/v1/subjects p95=3969ms — highest p95 variance of all API endpoints
evidence: S03 min=2831ms median=3233ms max=4417ms p95=3969ms; high range of 1586ms
root_cause_hypothesis: Subjects endpoint may perform a GROUP BY or aggregate query without proper index; first-request overhead from cold query plan cache; variable row count per request
recommendation: 1) Add composite index on subjects table (subject_category, active); 2) Cache subjects list in Redis with 5-minute TTL; 3) Review query plan via EXPLAIN ANALYZE

observation: /news endpoint returns HTTP 500 under test — cannot measure news module load performance
evidence: HTTP 500 confirmed from phase2 and phase3 pre-test check; error occurs before load testing
root_cause_hypothesis: Underlying error in news controller/view — likely missing DB record or template variable binding failure; see phase2 error log
recommendation: 1) Fix /news controller error before phase4 or production deployment; 2) Add integration test asserting /news returns 200 with expected content; 3) Check LibraryNews model seeder and DB migration

observation: /api/login endpoint times out (>10s) — cannot measure authentication flow under load
evidence: Timeout confirmed from phase2; /api/login not included in phase3 scenarios
root_cause_hypothesis: Laravel Sanctum token generation may be triggering slow hashing or DB query; possible misconfiguration of session/cookie driver in dev env
recommendation: 1) Profile /api/login with Xdebug; 2) Verify bcrypt cost factor is ≤12 in dev; 3) Check sanctum token cleanup queries; 4) Test /api/login separately with targeted profiling

observation: Endurance test (40 req/138s) shows consistent latency — no memory leak detected but no improvement over time
evidence: S09 avg=3454ms vs S01 avg=3443ms — delta=11ms; max=3713ms vs S01 max=3592ms — slight max increase
root_cause_hypothesis: PHP-FPM workers are long-lived enough to reuse opcode cache but no per-process query caching; stable behavior confirms no regression but also no warm-up benefit
recommendation: 1) Implement in-memory result caching (Redis) to show warm-path improvement; 2) Pre-warm PHP-FPM workers before load tests; 3) Endurance tests over longer window (>10 min) on staging to detect slow leaks
